scenarios.opening_query: The estate lawyer Mom hired put her house into the wrong kind of trust and now Medicaid says she's disqualified.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: General Litigation > Malpractice (Legal) || Wills & Trusts > Medicare/Medicaid Planning
scenarios.hidden_fact: The penalty window can still be cured — a planner can restructure within the lookback rules if we act this quarter; fixing eligibility first beats suing first.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The penalty can still be cured this quarter with restructuring — fixing eligibility comes before suing anybody.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether a planning cure is still available
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The cure window closed — she's paying $9,000 a month out of pocket because of his error, and the malpractice case is the recovery.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: The window closed — she's paying $9,000 a month because of his error. Malpractice is the recovery.

scenarios.opening_query: A dietitian's plan put my diabetic husband in the ICU and the clinic calls it 'individual variation.'
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: General Litigation > Malpractice (Medical) || Administrative Law > Professional Licensing
scenarios.hidden_fact: We discovered she isn't licensed at all — the 'RD' credentials are invented, and the board complaint against her and the clinic that employed her unlicensed is the first strike.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: She isn't even licensed — the 'RD' credential is invented. The board complaint against her and the clinic comes first.
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: She's fully credentialed — the case is her plan itself, which contradicted his endocrinologist's written orders in the chart.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: She's fully credentialed — the case is her plan contradicting the endocrinologist's written orders.
scenarios.vetting_note: Unlicensed-practice fact opens the regulatory route.

scenarios.opening_query: Our surveyor's mistake means part of our new garage sits on the neighbor's land.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: General Litigation > General Litigation || Real Property > General (Residential)
scenarios.hidden_fact: The neighbor is willing to sell us the sliver — papering that boundary adjustment and lot-line agreement is the immediate work; the surveyor's bill comes after.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The neighbor will sell us the sliver — the boundary adjustment is the immediate work, surveyor accountability after.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether a consensual boundary fix is available first
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The neighbor demands demolition and refuses all offers — the negligence case against the surveyor for the loss is the fight.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: The neighbor demands demolition — the surveyor negligence case is the fight.

scenarios.opening_query: The IME doctor the insurance sent me to wrote a report about an exam that never happened.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: General Litigation > Malpractice (Medical) || Consumer Law > Insurance
scenarios.hidden_fact: No doctor-patient relationship exists in an IME — the fabricated report is the insurer's instrument, and the bad-faith/fraud practice case against the carrier is where it bites.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: An IME creates no doctor-patient relationship — the fabricated report is the insurer's instrument, and the bad-faith case is where it bites.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the IME context redirects the claim to the insurer
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: This wasn't an IME — he was my own treating physician documenting visits that never occurred while billing me; the professional case is direct.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: He was my own treating physician billing for visits that never happened — the professional case is direct.

scenarios.opening_query: A midwife missed every warning sign and our birth story became an emergency instead of a homecoming.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: General Litigation > Malpractice (Medical) || General Litigation > Tort Claims Act
scenarios.hidden_fact: She's a certified nurse-midwife at the county health department's birth center — a public employee, which adds the government claim-notice deadline to the malpractice case.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: She's a CNM at the county health department's birth center — a public employee, so the claim-notice deadline stacks on the malpractice case.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the provider is a public employee
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: She's in private home-birth practice — a standard professional negligence case against her and her backup physician.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: She's private home-birth practice — standard negligence against her and the backup physician.
scenarios.vetting_note: Public-provider overlay; baby recovered — the harm framing stays on the emergency, not a loss.

scenarios.opening_query: Our wedding photographer lost every image from the ceremony and offered us $200 and an apology.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Consumer Law > General || Consumer Law > Small Claims Advice
scenarios.hidden_fact: His contract caps damages at the $3,400 fee — which is exactly small-claims range, and I'd happily bring that case myself with coaching.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: His contract caps damages at the $3,400 fee — small-claims range, and I'll bring it myself with coaching.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the capped amount fits self-help
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: There's no cap — and he's done this to four couples this year per the reviews; the consumer-practices case against his operation is bigger than our fee.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: No cap — and he's done it to four couples this year. The practices case is bigger than our fee.
